section.heading: Emergency leave of absence

subsection.heading: Emergency Leave of Absence.—
subsection.content: The Secretary concerned may grant a member of the armed forces emergency leave of absence for a qualifying emergency.

subsection.heading: Limitations.—
subsection.chapeau: An emergency leave of absence under this section—

paragraph.content: may be granted only once for any member;
may be granted only to prevent the member from entering unearned leave status or excess leave status; and
may not extend for a period of more than 14 days.

subsection.heading: Qualifying Emergency.—
subsection.chapeau: In this section, the term “qualifying emergency”, with respect to a member of the armed forces, means a circumstance that—

subparagraph.content: a medical condition of a member of the immediate family of the member; or
any other hardship that the Secretary concerned determines appropriate for purposes of this section; and

paragraph.content: is verified to the Secretary’s satisfaction based upon information or opinion from a source in addition to the member that the Secretary considers to be objective and reliable.

subsection.heading: Military Department Regulations.—
subsection.content: Regulations prescribed under this section by the Secretaries of the military department shall be as uniform as practicable and shall be subject to approval by the Secretary of Defense.

paragraph.content: The term “unearned leave status” means leave approved to be used by a member of the armed forces that exceeds the amount of leave credit that has been accrued as a result of the member’s active service and that has not been previously used by the member.

The term “excess leave status” means leave approved to be used by a member of the armed forces that is unearned leave for which a member is unable to accrue leave credit during the member’s current term of service before the member’s separation.

section.sourceCredit: (Added Pub. L. 107–314, div. A, title V, § 572(a), Dec. 2, 2002, 116 Stat. 2557.)
